title: Harden local Restic command surfaces against probe-as-write
goal: Move the active local Restic wrapper scripts under a canonical repository owner and make help, status and unknown-argument behavior fail closed so an inspection command cannot start backup, retention or prune work.

approval.note: Registration only. Runtime replacement or timer changes require a later reviewed authorization.

acceptance.assertion: Active Restic wrapper and unit sources are versioned under one explicit repository owner with install and rollback instructions.
--help and explicit status or plan modes perform no backup, retention, prune, restore or repository mutation.
Unknown arguments fail closed before locks, tunnels, sentinel writes or Restic commands are started.
Tests prove that help and unknown-argument probes cause zero backup-side effects and that normal scheduled execution retains its existing restore gate.
Any later runtime replacement is hash-bound, reversible and separately reviewed before timers or active scripts change.

metadata.incident_summary: A --help probe invoked two scripts that lacked argument parsing, creating encrypted snapshots and running one configured local retention/prune cycle.
metadata.operator_authorization: User instruction on 2026-07-11 to finish the Restic runtime verification and Bureau closeout; no maintenance, retention, forget or prune action authorized.

github_ci_observation.reason: GitHub account payment or spending-limit block; not classified as a code or test failure.

validation: Complete Infra make validate passed on the exact published PR #124 head in a fresh detached checkout.
All 24 Restic runtime tests passed; ShellCheck and git diff --check passed.
Help, status, plan and unknown-argument probes on both deployed wrappers changed neither marker nor sentinel, acquired no persistent lock, opened no tunnel and left no temporary files.
A real backup created one unique run-tagged full snapshot and restored the sentinel from exactly that snapshot with byte-identical SHA-256.
Installer, runtime verifier, daily timer, disabled maintenance timer, retirement verifier and rollback paths passed after the backup.

validated_acceptance.canonical-owner: Infra owns scripts, units, installer, verifier, install receipt and rollback contract; active runtime is bound to source commit 0d0cba93.
validated_acceptance.safe-help: Live --help, status and plan probes on backup and maintenance wrappers were non-mutating; marker and sentinel hashes and marker mtime remained unchanged.
validated_acceptance.unknown-args: Both deployed wrappers returned exit code 2 before lock, tunnel, Restic process, marker write or temporary file creation.
validated_acceptance.regression-tests: 24 focused tests plus the complete Infra suite prove probe safety and exact-snapshot restore binding; the real run returned BACKUP_OK restore_sentinel=PASS.
validated_acceptance.runtime-rollout: PRs #121-#123 implemented the hash-bound reversible runtime; PR #124 records post-rollout exact-snapshot evidence. Previous releases and backup directories remain available.

metadata.outcome: The local Restic command surfaces are canonically owned, probe-safe, commit-bound and reversible. A real Heimberry backup and exact-snapshot sentinel restore passed. Maintenance remains explicitly unauthorized.
metadata.self_review: PASS: implementation commits, deployed hashes, live no-side-effect probes, exact snapshot metadata, sentinel hashes, timer states, receipts and rollback directories were independently re-read.
